FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class FlipView

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

A WinUI FlipView: a clipped frame showing exactly one item at a time, with thin acrylic navigation bars hugging the frame edges (no dot/pip indicator — WinUI FlipView has none). Selection CLAMPS at the ends (MoveNext / MovePrevious, FlipView_Partial.cpp:131-139/:165-171 — no wrap-around) and the end bar is not rendered (nothingPrevious/nothingNext, :1242-1278). The bars are hidden until pointer movement / keyboard focus shows them, then fade out after FLIP_VIEW_BUTTONS_SHOW_DURATION_MS = 3000ms (FlipView_Partial.h:13). Adjacent selection changes slide (UseTouchAnimationsForAllNavigation, default TRUE — FlipView_Partial.cpp:1639-1645); non-adjacent changes jump (SetOffsetToSelectedIndex, :1719). Wheel flips one item per distinct event (200ms throttle, FlipView_Partial.h:10), arrow keys navigate (Selector::HandleNavigationKey, Selector_Partial.cpp:3694-3773), and the content pans with a drag, snapping MandatorySingle on release (ScrollingHost snap points, FlipView_themeresources.xaml:297). All items are realized (no virtualization — WinUI's VirtualizingStackPanel is out of scope for this control surface).

public static class FlipView

Methods

Create(IReadOnlyList<Element>, float, float, int, Action<int>?, bool, bool)

Arbitrary per-item content (the WinUI FlipViewItem surface — FlipViewItem_themeresources.xaml:23-25). Each item fills its page cell (FlipViewItem Horizontal/VerticalContentAlignment = Stretch, :17-18). selectedIndex is live-controlled: a changed value navigates the view (the Selector SelectedIndex DP, FlipView_Partial.cpp:1607-1613); internal navigation reports through onSelectionChanged (SelectionChanged).

public static Element Create(IReadOnlyList<Element> items, float width = 400, float height = 240, int selectedIndex = 0, Action<int>? onSelectionChanged = null, bool useTouchAnimationsForAllNavigation = true, bool vertical = false)

Parameters

items IReadOnlyList<Element>
width float
height float
selectedIndex int
onSelectionChanged Action<int>
useTouchAnimationsForAllNavigation bool
vertical bool

Returns

Element

Create(IReadOnlyList<string>, float, float, int, Action<int>?, bool, bool)

String-item convenience: each entry renders centered at 20px primary text.

public static Element Create(IReadOnlyList<string> items, float width = 400, float height = 240, int selectedIndex = 0, Action<int>? onSelectionChanged = null, bool useTouchAnimationsForAllNavigation = true, bool vertical = false)

Element
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
